exemplar.title: To what extent does St Thomas Aquinasâs cosmological argument withstand criticisms by atheists/agnostics?

detailedExemplarData.title: To what extent does St Thomas Aquinasâs cosmological argument withstand criticisms by atheists/agnostics?

files.fileName: An evaluation of the utility of arguments for God.pdf

strengths: Clear and accurate communication of topic with comprehensive explanation of Aquinasâs three âWaysâ
Explicit, wellâfocused research question consistently drives the structure and discussion
Conclusion succinctly recaps findings and reinforces a coherent answer to the research question
weaknesses: Introduction offers broad historical context that occasionally drifts from the research focus
Methodological rationale for source selection is only partly developed
Reliance on nonâpeerâreviewed website material reduces academic rigor
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand assessments indicate two band-5 performances and one band-4. The topic is communicated accurately and the research question is consistently referenced, but methodological justification and source selection breadth are only partially developed. This merits a strong 5 of 6.

strengths: Coherent application of key quotations from Aquinas, Hume, and Smart to illustrate arguments
Clear and logical sequencing of subject knowledge about contingency, causation, motion, and fallacy of composition
weaknesses: Frequent misspellings of âAquinasâ undermine precision in terminology
Some paraphrases are superficial and a few assertions lack citation (e.g., Big Bang reference)
Occasional conceptual inaccuracies (e.g., conflation of âatemporal causationâ with the First Way)
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: With two strands at band 4 and one at band 3, the work shows mostly relevant and appropriate use of sources with coherent knowledge but inconsistent terminology control. A mid-band score of 4 out of 6 reflects these strengths and limitations.

strengths: Good selection of critics directly targeting each of Aquinasâs Ways, demonstrating research relevance
Clear, logically structured analyses with well-supported conclusions and occasional deep insights (e.g., analogies, deity-neutrality observation)
Balanced critical evaluation with recognition of counter-rebuttals such as Hick vs Smart
weaknesses: Critical evaluation is sometimes superficial, lacking sustained probing critique of key objections
Engagement with modern scientific perspectives is brief and underdeveloped
Depth of analysis varies across sections, leaving some assertions thinly evidenced
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The essay shows good research relevance and coherent analysis (strands in the 7-9 band) but uneven depth and occasional superficial critique (lower end of that band). An 8 of 12 accurately reflects solid but not exemplary critical thinking.

strengths: Overall structure is logical and includes required elements (title page, table of contents, section headings)
Headings and section divisions generally guide the reader through each argument and critique
weaknesses: Page numbers are missing, impairing navigation
Inconsistent heading hierarchy and split URLs in bibliography violate layout conventions
Citation style is inconsistent, and key layout elements (e.g. TOC page references) are absent
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Structure is appropriate but not exemplary (band 3), while layout conventions are only partly applied (band 1â2). A combined holistic score of 3 out of 4 balances the logical organization against significant formatting lapses.

strandSelections.reasoning: The research questionââTo what extent does St Thomas Aquinasâs cosmological argument withstand criticisms by atheists/agnostics?ââis explicit, focused and consistently referenced throughout the body of the essay.  Each substantive section is organised around assessing one of Aquinasâs three arguments against specific named critics, demonstrating a strong connection between the question and the ensuing discussion.

Philosophical terminology (contingent, necessary, infinite regress, actuality/potentiality) is generally accurate, but frequent misspellings of âAquinasâ and occasional mis-uses (âopposeâ for âopposedâ) show inconsistency.  Concepts are understood yet the language control is not fully sustained, warranting the lower end of the 3-4 band.

The title, research question and introductory paragraph clearly situate the essay within Philosophy of Religion and specify that the focus will be Aquinasâs cosmological argument vis-Ã -vis atheist/agnostic objections.  The student succinctly explains each of the three âWaysâ that will be examined and signals how the critical discussion will proceed.  Minor spelling slips (e.g. âAguinasâ) do not impede understanding, so communication remains accurate and largely comprehensive.

The title, subject, session and word count are given, satisfying some layout requirements; however, page numbers are absent and several URLs in the bibliography are split.  Citation style is inconsistent.  Consequently, only some layout conventions are correctly applied.

A logical structure is evident: title page, table of contents, section headings for each Way/critic and a conclusion.  Required elements (abstract is no longer mandatory) are present, but occasional repetition and some abrupt paragraphing indicate that the organisation, while appropriate, is not exemplary.

Arguments are traced and objections noted, but critical evaluation is sometimes superficialâe.g. the dismissal of Leeâs rationalist critique or of Humeâs fallacy without full justification.  While the discussion is coherent, it lacks sustained, probing critique characteristic of higher bands, meriting the lower end of 4-6.

Primary sources (Aquinas, Hume) and recognised secondary texts (Hick, Vardy, Smart) are included, showing an informed if modest range.  Website material is used to supplement explanations but occasionally lacks academic weight.  Overall selection is relevant but breadth and critical justification of sources are only partly developed, placing the work securely in the 3-4 band.

The research selected is appropriate and mostly relevant to answering the stated question; each critic chosen directly targets one of Aquinasâs Ways.  Nonetheless, coverage is unevenâe.g. modern scientific perspectives are only briefly alluded toâso overall quality is good rather than excellent.

The student demonstrates clear understanding of contingency, causation, motion, and the âfallacy of compositionâ.  Explanations are coherent and sequenced logically, though occasional inaccuracies (e.g. equating âatemporal causationâ with the First Way) and stylistic lapses limit the depth of treatment.  Source use is partially effective, aligning with the middle band.

The essay offers clear, logically structured analyses of each argument and presents a final judgement that the cosmological argument âsurvives only oneâ criticism.  Support is provided by textual references and the student occasionally weighs counter-rebuttals (e.g. Hick vs Smart).  Depth of analysis varies and some assertions are thinly evidenced, keeping the work in the mid-band.

Quotations from Aquinasâs Summa, Humeâs Dialogues and Smartâs essays are woven into the narrative and generally used appropriately to illustrate points under discussion.  However, some paraphrases are superficial and a few claims are left uncited (e.g. Big Bang reference), so application is mostlyâbut not alwaysâwell-chosen and relevant.

annotations.comment: Including the word count at the outset meets formatting requirements and enhances transparency regarding compliance with the 4 000-word limit.
The table of contents is present but lacks page numbers, impairing navigability and missing a key layout convention.
The title clearly communicates the studyâs focus and situates it within Philosophy of Religion, demonstrating effective topic communication and comprehensive explanation.
The research question is explicit, wellâfocused and consistently referenced, effectively driving the structure and discussion throughout the essay.
Frequent misspelling of âAquinasâ (e.g., âAguinasâ) distracts from the argument and undermines precision in philosophical terminology.
Present the first-cause argument in formal premiseâconclusion format (e.g. numbering each premise) to clarify its logical structure.
Phrasing error: âoppose to reasonâ should read âas opposed to reasonâ; revise for clarity and formal tone.
The opening paragraph offers broad historical context but drifts from the stated research question; narrowing the focus early would strengthen the introduction.
The methodological paragraph would benefit from a concise rationale for source selection, explicitly linking each author to aspects of the research question.
The use of heading hierarchy is inconsistent here; ensure h2, h3 tags align to maintain a coherent structure.
Contextualize Humeâs quotation within a broader summary of the âDialoguesâ to avoid misinterpretation and show depth of understanding.
Reliance on a nonâpeer-reviewed blog (Feser, 2010) diminishes academic rigor; prioritize scholarly journal articles or books.
The explanation of potentiality and actuality is accurate and clearly underpins the motion argument, reflecting coherent subject knowledge.
The mention of the Big Bang theory lacks a supporting citation; add a reliable source when referencing modern scientific perspectives.
Smartâs critique is introduced clearly but would benefit from deeper engagement with alternative scholarly interpretations.
The wall analogy vividly illustrates the fallacy of composition, strengthening the evaluation of Humeâs criticism.
Maintain consistent use of apostrophes in possessives (e.g. âHumeâsâ), which supports clarity and correct formatting.
Recognizing that the argument could equally support other deities shows strong critical insight into its inability to uniquely validate the Christian God.
The claim that inanimate factors could initiate motion is speculative; support such assertions with empirical or theoretical sources.
The conclusion accurately recaps each Way and the critical findings, reinforcing a coherent answer to the research question.
The report lacks a reflective section on the studentâs own decision-making and planning, missing an opportunity to demonstrate engagement.
Bibliography entries show inconsistent citation style and split URLs, hindering credibility and access to sources.
Several URLs in the bibliography are broken across lines; ensure each link is contiguous to maintain functionality.
Page numbers are missing throughout the document, which impedes reader navigation and violates layout conventions.
